hero.title: Welcome to the DFREE® Financial Freedom Movement
hero.subtitle: Empower yourself with practical tools and community-driven strategies. Transform your economic future through education, support, and collective action.
hero.bookAlt: DFREE Say Yes to No Debt book cover
hero.videoLabel: Background video of the DFREE financial freedom movement

whoWeAre.title: Who we are
whoWeAre.introRest: is a tax exempt, 501C3 organization advancing financial wellness, economic empowerment, and self-sufficiency in underserved communities by delivering free, community-centered initiatives that help families build lasting financial confidence and freedom.
whoWeAre.mission: The DFREE® Global Foundation ensures that financial freedom is not a privilege. By subsidizing access to DFREE's proven tools and curriculum - and through signature initiatives including FinFe$t, community campaigns, the Billion Dollar Challenge, the BottomUp Leadership Institute, and community development projects in Africa - the Foundation brings the resources of the DFREE® movement within reach of every family, regardless of income.
whoWeAre.vision: As the philanthropic arm of the Movement, the DFREE Global Foundation is committed to expanding access and impact by reaching underserved communities, removing barriers to financial education, and connecting individuals to the resources and support necessary to build lasting financial empowerment.

pillars.title: Pillars
pillars.subtitle: Creative solutions crafted to tackle financial hurdles. We aim to overcome your monetary challenges.

billionDollarChallenge.name: Billion Dollar Challenge
billionDollarChallenge.description: The Billion Dollar Challenge (BDC) is a digital initiative of the DFREE Global Foundation that helps individuals and organizations reduce debt and build savings through goal tracking, behavioral motivation, and community accountability, while providing partners with measurable, data-driven impact.

dfreeAfrica.name: DFREE® In Africa
dfreeAfrica.description: The DFREE Global Foundation is deeply committed to advancing financial literacy, leadership development, and community empowerment across the African continent through strategic partnerships and targeted investments.

communityCampaigns.name: Community Campaigns
communityCampaigns.description: The DFREE Global Foundation leads community-wide campaigns that unite churches, civic groups, and local organizations to deliver coordinated financial empowerment programs, fostering shared learning, accountability, and collective celebration through structured launches, weekly sessions, and culminating graduation events.

finfest.name: FinFE$T®: Where Wealth Meets Wellness
finfest.description: FinFe$t® is DFREE®'s flagship community event that blends financial education, health, and wellness through engaging experiences to equip participants with practical tools to drive holistic well-being and long-term prosperity. It’s 100% free.

accessScholarships.name: DFREE® Access Scholarships
accessScholarships.description: The DFREE® Scholarship program provides need-based support to remove financial barriers to accessing DFREE® content – educational resources, digital learning, live training, free financial counseling and limited emergency assistance to help members of our community pursue debt reduction and lasting financial freedom.

ourImpact.label: Our impact

ourImpact.description: Every milestone represents lives transformed and a step closer to financial freedom.

experience.title: The <primary>FinFE$T</primary> Experience
experience.paragraph1: FinFE$T focused on equipping families with valuable financial insights and practical strategies by providing over 60 Free Resources including health screening stations by RWJBarnabas Health (RWJBH), private financial consultations by Prudential’s Financial professionals, credit counseling by GreenPath.
experience.paragraph2: Live presentations and breakout sessions by subject matter experts, as well as books and resources from several other partners – real time solutions all under one roof to support the overall well-being of our community. Additionally, FinFE$T featured a Job Fair and Resource Fair that connected attendees with employment opportunities and essential services to help them thrive.

hero.title: Unlock Your Financial Freedom Journey
hero.subtitle: The DFREE® Access Scholarship offers free access to courses, materials, coaching, and events, helping participants achieve financial stability and empowerment.

about.title: What are Access Scholarships?
about.subtitle: About the Program
about.description:
The DFREE® Access Scholarship program removes financial barriers that prevent individuals and families from participating in DFREE® financial freedom education. Access scholarships are essentially fee waivers or financial assistance that cover the costs of DFREE® programs, tools, events, and access to financial professionals.

Programming includes the DFREE® Online Academy, 12 Steps to Financial Freedom curriculum, Say Yes to No Debt books and workbooks, podcasts and webinars. Fee assistance is supported by corporate partners (or sponsors).

whyScholarshipsMatter.title: Why Scholarships Matter?

hero.title: Community Campaigns
hero.subtitle: Dynamic community-wide campaigns that unite churches, civic groups, and local organizations to implement synchronized DFREE® financial empowerment programs. These campaigns are designed to create a collective impact by promoting shared learning, mutual accountability, and community celebration.

about.title: What are community campaigns
about.description: Community campaigns bring together entire neighborhoods to tackle financial challenges as one. Unlike isolated programs, these coordinated efforts create momentum, accountability, and lasting transformation.

impact.title: DFREE® Day of Impact
impact.subtitle: A Community Activation Model for Financial Freedom, Collaboration and Collective Action
impact.description: The DFREE® Day of Impact is a new initiative that features a one-day, multi-touch community engagement experience designed to introduce DFREE® to a local community, deepen relationships with key leaders, and seed long-term collaboration that leads to measurable financial empowerment outcomes. It brings together nonprofits, faith leaders, civic stakeholders, entrepreneurs, and residents into the launch of an ongoing DFREE® ecosystem in the host community.

unifiedCollaboration.title: Unified collaboration
unifiedCollaboration.description: Multiple organizations work in concert, sharing resources and learning from one another. This creates a network effect that strengthens every participant.

sharedAccountability.title: Shared accountability
sharedAccountability.description: Weekly touchpoints and group learning sessions keep participants engaged and motivated. Progress is celebrated together, making the journey feel less isolating.

measurableImpact.title: Measurable impact

hero.title: Empowering Africa
hero.subtitle: Driving financial freedom and sustainable community development through education, skills training and economic programs.

ourStory.title: our story
ourStory.subtitle: Transforming financial landscapes across African communities
ourStory.description: Driven by a vision to empower the African diaspora, Dr. DeForest B. Soaries, Jr. founded Dfree Global Foundation, which birthed DFREE® Africa, to replace the cycle of poverty with a mindset of wealth and self-sufficiency. This mission is deeply rooted in Dr. Soaries’ long-standing connection to the continent, beginning with his first visit to South Africa in 1990. He has since spent decades forging continental partnerships that champion economic, social, and spiritual growth, dedicating his leadership to building strategic connections across the continent, especially in Ghana, Liberia, Uganda, Botswana, South Africa and several other countries. His work focuses on integrating economic development with educational, spiritual and social progress to create sustainable, higher standards of living for all.

banner.label: Learn how you can support

pageInfo.descText: To mark the 10th anniversary of his first visit to Ghana, Dr. Soaries was enstooled a chief by His Majesty the King of Akyem Abuakwa, in recognition of his work towards development throughout the kingdom and the country. As Nkosuorhene (chief of development), he continues to drive projects that directly address community especially in education, livelihoods for children and youth and community development.

workplace.title: DFREE IN THE WORKPLACE
workplace.description:
Through a local church, the DFREE Club started an outreach to enroll the youth in the Billion Dollar challenge (BDC).  This comprised mostly college students and new graduates with entry level jobs and some student loans. In its first year alone, the first group recruited over 20 members who paid down about twenty-thousand cedis in debt (about $4,200) – a substantial amount by Ghanaian standard.
 The church-based DFREE activities also outreached to traders in local markets, mostly women who trade in fast moving consumable goods. Through monthly engagements at their places of business, the Club’s objective was to help them understand basic bookkeeping and encourage them to save in banking institutions in order to grow their business and improve their standards of living.

church.title: DFREE IN THE CHURCH
church.description:
The DFREE program was  piloted in Ghana within an organization where workplace loans were  common practice.  Without a cap on borrowing power of employees,  employers capitalized on this practice to leave most employees in wage  deficit at the end of the pay cycle.
 Under the leadership of  Mark, several staff of the organization joined the club and set  individual as well as communal goals to pay off debt using such  strategies such as the snowball method taught in the program. This  kicked off and spiraled into a campaign even without the participants’  access to workbooks.
 More importantly, they  formed a support group that motivated and encouraged one another by  sharing success stories and personal approaches to self-discipline.  In  short, a lifestyle and mindset change around money and spending was  born.

partnerships.title: PARTNERSHIPS
partnerships.description:
The DFREE Club, Ghana has partnered with Gamma Alpha Sigma Zeta chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. (A.K.A. Ghana Zetas) to promote financial literary among specific groups of  consumers.  This mostly includes women and girls in shelters and halfway homes often rescued or escaping from abusive homes. They also work with fisherfolks and farmers in rural communities, and street hawkers in  urban areas. 
Working through the Agape  Sanctuary of Victory Bible Church, the engagement of youth in the Billion Dolla Challenge  and outreach to market traders have been extremely successful campaigns  that can only group through other sanctuaries of the church.

challenges.title: CHALLENGES
challenges.description:
The DFREE Club, Ghana has  struggled with sustainability over the years.  Funding for travel,  access to resources like books and internet service are some of the  factors affecting the few volunteers who have committed to driving the  cause.  
In this new phase, the  emphasis on working through the church, hopefully, changes the  narrative.  Support in the form of books and funding to host events,  educate market traders and reach more beneficiaries would be welcome.

successes.title: SUCCESSES
successes.description:
Over 100 individuals have successfully  completed the program since inception. Apart from the debt paid down,  group members have also saved over 60,000 cedis ($8,000) collectively in about 3 years through the Billion Dollar Challenge. 
The Club continues to lead  the charge of helping individuals pay down debt, save to create wealth,  and learn to invest and grow their incomes for better standards of  living.

featuredProjects.subtitle: Featured projects
featuredProjects.description: With the support of dedicated partners and community leaders, our initiatives drive measurable impact and create lasting change across Ghana.

pageInfo.descText_Quote: “Our lives continue to be  blessed by the work we do at DFREE®. It is an honor we cherish greatly  that God can use us for such a big mandate of helping people live in  financial freedom.”

partnerships.title: PARTNERSHIPS
partnerships.description:
Karani Leadership – This is a successful black-owned company offering business and strengths  coaching for entrepreneurs, and executive coaching for senior  management. The partnership gave birth to the incorporation of a 4-week  DFREE course in their entrepreneurs’ program. 

Lucha Lunako – dfree South Africa teamed up this prominent youth development not-for-profit  organization to enhance their Financial Behavior Improvement division,  drawing from the DFREE Young Money curriculum.

Pastor Debbie Bruce and the Baptist Convention of Southern Africa (BCSA) – Ps. Bruce is the vice president of the Convention who has fostered a  relationship to train pastors and church leaders in the Convention.  Through the partnership, train-the-trainer sessions are also held for  George East Ministers Fraternity so they can in turn train other leaders to launch the dfree program in their churches.

challenges.title: CHALLENGES
challenges.description: In-person training sessions were curtailed due to the COVID-19 restrictions, and it has been hard to reactivate. Financial constraints on participants due to the high rate of job losses since 2020/2021 translates to competing necessities on their stimulus income from government. Internet service is not a priority on the needs list, meaning attendance at virtual events are also low. Sadly, those most affected are also the bracket of people highly indebted who need DFREE® the most.

successes.title: SUCCESSES
successes.description: Some notable milestones achieved by  DFREE South Africa include starting savings clubs, nurturing  subsistence entrepreneurs and cooperatives in underserved South African  communities that have emerged. We believe in continued and extensive  behavioral financial literacy research.

featuredProjects.subtitle: Featured projects
featuredProjects.description: With the support of dedicated partners and community leaders, our initiatives drive measurable impact and create lasting change across South Africa.

pageInfo.descText:
DFREE®’s initiatives in Uganda started in 2019 when Dr. Soaries was the guest of His Highness Bishop John Chrisostom Wayabire, king of the Bugwere kingdom, and the first known African traditional ruler who is also the Bishop of a Christian church. At the 5th coronation anniversary of His Highness Wayabire, Dr. Soaries had the opportunity to meet the president of the country and hold council with members of the community, especially the Queen’s special women’s initiative that was providing skills training and livelihoods to women and girls in deprived communities.
DFREE® has developed an even stronger working relationship with King Geoffrey Wayabire, who succeeded his father as king in 2023. The partnership has birthed 2 key projects:

theCommitment.title: THE COMMITMENT
theCommitment.description: Dr. Soaries pledged to raise funds for 10 borehole wells for 10 communities. Unlike ponds, the wells tap into a deep underground aquifer hundreds of feet underground, protected from surface contaminants.

theImpact.title: THE IMPACT - A RIPPLE EFFECT
theImpact.description:
In December 2025, the first 2 wells pumped clean water in two villages, marking the transition from a livestock-shared pond to a dedicated well that represented more than just health; it represented dignity. The benefits of the well reached far beyond just quenching thirst:
Health: Incidence of ailments are expected to drop dramatically within a few months.

points.point1: Education: Children, particularly girls, who were previously tasked with hauling water from the pond, will now be able to attend school consistently.
points.point2: Economic Time-Savings: What used to be a two-hour daily chore of trekking to fetch water and boil it is now a five-minute walk to a reliable pump.
points.point3: Hygiene: All household chores, including laundry, are also done with clean water, thereby reducing the instance of contamination from livestock activity.

theImpact.cta: Help support this cause to provide clean drinking water to the villages of Bugwere.

featuredProjects.subtitle: Featured projects
featuredProjects.description: With the support of dedicated partners and community leaders, our initiatives drive measurable impact and create lasting change across Uganda.

hero.title: DFREE® BottomUp Leadership Institute
hero.subtitle: Lasting change rises from "bottom up", not the top down.

aboutTheInstitute.label: About the Institute
aboutTheInstitute.title: Grassroots leaders equipped to transform lives and neighborhoods
aboutTheInstitute.description: The DFREE® Bottom Up Leadership Institute identifies community leaders ready to create change and equips them with the knowledge, strategy, and tools to do it. Through cohort based learning that blends online courses, immersive retreats, and real world site visits, participants emerge as trained leaders with sustainable, replicable plans for their communities. By investing in people at the community level, the Institute multiplies impact, preparing a new generation of trainers and leaders to carry the DFREE® mission forward and build a more just and equitable economy from the ground up.

info2.label: COHORT
info2.title: Based learning
info2.description: Train alongside a community of peers

info3.label: BLENDED
info3.title: Modes of learning
info3.description: Online courses, retreats & site visits to best-practice orgs

whoIsAFellow.label: Who is a DFREE® Fellow?
whoIsAFellow.title: A leader well equipped for the community
whoIsAFellow.description: Every leader who completes the Bottom-Up Leadership Institute is formally recognized as a DFREE® Fellow — a credential signifying mastery across financial literacy, financial empowerment, and economic justice. DFREE® Fellows are authorized to carry the DFREE® curriculum into their communities, train others, and serve as credentialed ambassadors of equitable economic transformation. The DFREE® Fellow designation is a mark of rigor, trust, and community accountability — recognized by partner institutions, sponsors, and municipalities nationwide.

coreAreas.label: Three Core Areas
coreAreas.title: The Institute’s pillars of expertise
coreAreas.description: Every participant develops deep, applicable knowledge across three interconnected domains — each building on the last

pillar1.pillarTitle: Building the foundation of money knowledge
pillar1.pillarDescription: Foundational understanding of money management, debt elimination, credit, and wealth-building principles — with targeted emphasis on nonprofit and micro-business leaders driving community impact.

pillar2.pillarDescription: Equipping individuals and families with actionable strategies to take meaningful control of their economic futures — moving from financial survival to freedom and generational wealth.

pillar3.pillarDescription: Preparing leaders who can identify, address, and respond to systemic economic inequities in their communities — transforming awareness into advocacy and advocacy into lasting structural change.

developLeaders.label: How do we develop leaders?
developLeaders.title: A blended, immersive learning model
developLeaders.description: The Institute uses a cohort-based approach that immerses participants in proven content and real-world strategies — not just theory, but transformation in action.

model1.title: Online courses & presentations
model1.description: Structured digital curriculum covering all three core areas designed for community leaders balancing full lives and full commitment to change.

model2.title: Immersive retreats
model2.description: Deep-dive gatherings that build community among cohort members and deepen content mastery — creating the relational foundation essential to leadership.

model3.title: Site visits to best-practice orgs
model3.description: Real-world visits to organizations modeling successful financial empowerment work — connecting participants to proven strategies and inspiring networks.

developLeaders.quote: "By investing in people at the community level, the Institute multiplies impact  preparing a new generation of trainers and leaders to carry the DFREE® mission forward."

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: The Bottom-Up Leadership Institute doesn’t just build individual capacity  it multiplies impact. Every graduate returns equipped with sustainable, replicable plans and the credibility to lead real change

work1.title: Identify
work1.description: Community leaders with proven commitment and local trust are identified and recruited into cohort programs.

work2.title: Train
work2.description: Participants complete the blended learning journey online courses, retreats, and site visits over the program term.

work3.title: Graduate
work3.description: Graduates earn the DFREE® Fellow designation, return with sustainable plans, DFREE® tools, and the authority to train others in their communities.

work4.title: Multiply
work4.description: Each leader replicates the model, building a more just and equitable economy from the ground up.

supportTheInstitute.label: Support the  institute
supportTheInstitute.title: Invest in the Next Generation of Leaders
supportTheInstitute.description: Your partnership helps expand access to training, resources, and real-world experiences that create lasting change.
supportTheInstitute.imageAlt: The Leadership Institute Support Section Image

banner.title: Start Leading Change Today
banner.description: Join a movement committed to building financially empowered communities from the ground up.

success.title: Application successful!
success.body: Your application was successfully submitted, our team will review your submission and contact you on next steps of this process

errors.submitFailed: We couldn't submit your application. Please try again in a moment.

covenant.title: DFREE® Fellow Alumni Covenant
covenant.intro: The DFREE® Fellowship is not a transaction — it is a covenant. As a Fellow, you receive full access to programming, mentorship, and a national network at no cost. In return, the DFREE Foundation asks each Fellow to sustain this mission beyond graduation:

commitments.capstone: Complete and present your Capstone Community Deployment Project by the cohort's end date.
commitments.alumniNetwork: Remain active in the DFREE® Fellow Alumni Network for a minimum of two years post-graduation.
commitments.trainCommunity: Train a minimum of 5 community members in DFREE® financial literacy principles within 12 months of graduation.
commitments.mentorApplicant: Serve as a reference or mentor for at least one future Fellow applicant from your community.
commitments.annualReport: Report annually on the reach and impact of your community deployment work to the DFREE Foundation.
commitments.representCredential: Represent the DFREE® Fellow credential with integrity and in alignment with DFREE® brand and mission values.

covenant.disclaimer: This covenant is not a legal obligation — it is a shared commitment to the communities we both serve.

certification.title: Applicant certification & submission
certification.body: By signing and submitting this application, I certify that all information is accurate. I understand that misrepresentation may result in disqualification. I accept the terms of the DFREE® Fellow Alumni Covenant and commit, if selected, to fully participate in the program and to deploy what I learn in service to my community.

fields.signatureHint: PNG, JPG, WEBP, or PDF up to 5MB

step1.title: About You & Your Work
step1.subtitle: Tell us about yourself and the work you do in your community.

fields.yearsServed: How long have you served this community?
fields.communityServed: Which community do you primarily serve?
fields.organizationDescription: Tell us about your organization and the people you serve

placeholders.organizationDescription: What does your organization do and who does it serve?

step2.title: Community Impact
step2.subtitle: Help us understand your experience and why this work matters to you.

fields.financialLiteracyExperience: Have you led or participated in financial literacy, wealth-building, or economic empowerment programs?
fields.programDescription: What was the program? Who did it serve? What impact did it have?
fields.communityChallenges: What financial challenges do people in your community face most often?
fields.motivation: Why do you want to become a DFREE® Fellow?

placeholders.programDescription: What was the program, who did it reach, and what was the outcome?
placeholders.communityChallenges: Describe the financial barriers, inequities, or needs you observe most frequently.

encouragement.title: You're halfway there
encouragement.body: This section helps us understand your heart for community transformation.

step3.title: Community Deployment Vision
step3.subtitle: Every DFREE® Fellow completes a Capstone Community Deployment Project as the culminating requirement of the fellowship. You will design, execute, and present a plan for bringing DFREE® financial literacy and empowerment programming to a defined audience in your community. This section asks for your initial concept—it will be developed fully during the fellowship.

capstone.title: What is a Capstone Community Deployment Project?
capstone.body: A structured plan to train 5–25 community members in DFREE® financial literacy and empowerment principles. It includes a defined audience, a delivery method, a timeline, and a simple impact measurement approach. Fellows present their capstone to the cohort and DFREE Foundation at the conclusion of the program.

fields.communityStory: Describe a moment when you witnessed financial hardship or economic injustice in your community.
fields.whoToTrain: Who would you like to train?
fields.peopleToReach: Approximately how many people would you like to reach?
fields.participationHistory: Have you led or participated in financial literacy, wealth-building, or economic empowerment programs?

placeholders.communityStory: What happened? Who was affected? What did you learn? What would you have liked to do?
placeholders.whoToTrain: e.g. single mothers at my church, youth 18–24, small business owners in my neighborhood

fields.cohortTerm: Which cohort term are you applying for?
fields.commitment: Are you able to commit to the full program?
fields.schedulingConstraints: Any scheduling constraints or accommodations needed?
